Errors documented: Fixed before this pass: PairingBroker.accept failures were only logged to Console, leaving no visible owner feedback. Compact incoming-pairing rows could also truncate process names or bundle IDs without exposing the full requester text. PairingBroker.accept could also create duplicate client profiles from a double-click or stale callback because it did not prove the request still had an active pending entry and was not already being accepted before persisting config. Quick Accept also passed nil for the profile label, so a CLI-provided display label could be visible in the prompt but missing from the accepted profile. PairingBroker.reject also unconditionally wrote a rejected outcome, so a stale Reject callback could overwrite an already accepted pairing outcome after the profile had been created. PairingBroker.accept also did not prune expired requests before checking pending state, so a stale Accept callback could create a profile after the pairing window elapsed. Deterministic incoming-pairing coverage now verifies prompt copy, expiry filtering, full requester row help, accept profile creation, requester-label persistence, expired accept rejection, stale/duplicate accept rejection, stale Reject preservation of terminal outcomes, accept failure feedback, and reject outcome.
Fix status: Fixed; deterministic incoming-pairing prompt and broker coverage added; compact requester rows expose full process/bundle/code hover help; expired, stale, and duplicate in-flight accepts now fail before creating another profile; stale Reject callbacks no longer overwrite accepted or rejected terminal outcomes; Quick Accept now persists the verified requester display name as the default profile label
